name: warm_day_forecast_improves_guard_dormant
description: Warm morning with a ceiling breach predicted at the 8 AM classification cycle. Weather improves (cool front arrives) by noon — outdoor drops 12F, ODE no longer predicts a breach. Guard goes dormant on the noon cycle. HVAC stays off. If a window is opened in the afternoon when outdoor drops below indoor, nat vent activates normally.
notes: Covers the 'weather-change resilience' pattern: guard evaluates on each 30-min cycle, not just at classification time.
The single-stateless-check design (no _ceiling_precool_scheduled flag) means the guard automatically goes dormant
when _find_ceiling_breach_time() returns None after forecast improvement.
The simulator_support=false assertions document the guard state at 8 AM (would fire) and noon (dormant).
The simulator CAN verify: classification_applied at 8 AM, natural_ventilation in the afternoon.
The simulator CANNOT verify: ceiling guard evaluation (requires ODE curve), guard dormant state transition.
This scenario directly tests the 'forecast improves mid-day' resilience described in the plan D5 decision.
Must be validated in production before promotion to golden/.

events.note: Morning: indoor 71F, outdoor 66F. Warm day forecast ahead.
Warm-day classification: hvac_mode=off, windows recommended 6–10 AM. ODE curve at this point predicts indoor hitting 74F by 2 PM — ceiling guard would evaluate.
8 AM: outdoor still tracking warm forecast. ODE predicts breach. Guard would be within lead_time window (120-min fallback) at this cycle.
11 AM: cool front arrived — outdoor dropped from 70F to 58F. ODE now predicts indoor will stay below 74F all day. Guard evaluates on this cycle and finds no breach — goes dormant. HVAC stays off.
2 PM: outdoor 61F well below indoor 73F. No breach on ODE forecast — guard dormant.
User opens door. outdoor=61F < indoor=73F, indoor=73F > comfort_heat=70F, outdoor <= threshold 77F — nat vent activates.

assertions.reason: Warm-day classification applied: hvac_mode=off. Indoor=71F > comfort_heat=70F — no comfort-floor guard needed.

assertions.note: At the 8 AM 30-min cycle, ODE predicted_indoor curve shows breach at ~2 PM. With k_active_cool=None → 120-min fallback, hours_to_breach (~6h) is OUTSIDE lead_time (2h), so guard is dormant at 8 AM. Guard would only fire when breach is within 2h. This assertion documents expected evaluation logic.
assertions.reason: 8 AM cycle: breach predicted at 2 PM (6h away), outside 120-min lead time — guard evaluates but does NOT fire yet.

assertions.note: At the 11 AM cycle, cool front has dropped outdoor from 70F to 58F. _find_ceiling_breach_time() returns None because ODE curve no longer crosses comfort_cool=74F. Guard goes dormant — no AC fires. HVAC stays off. This is the stateless re-evaluation advantage: no flag to cancel, no scheduled callback to remove.
assertions.reason: Forecast improved (outdoor -12F) → ODE predicts no breach → guard dormant on next cycle → HVAC stays off. No cancellation logic required.
sensor_open: outdoor=61F < indoor=73F, indoor=73F > comfort_heat=70F, outdoor=61F <= threshold 77F — nat vent activates normally after guard goes dormant.

verdict.summary: When forecast improves mid-day and ODE no longer predicts a breach, the ceiling guard goes dormant automatically on the next 30-min cycle
verdict.observed_behavior: N/A — this scenario tests new Issue #136 behavior not yet implemented
verdict.expected_behavior: Guard stateless re-evaluation: breach disappears from ODE curve → guard dormant → HVAC stays off → nat vent resumes in afternoon
